title: Morocco vs Haiti - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Morocco 4-2 in a chaotic first half that saw Haiti lead twice, before Morocco's greater quality took over. Saibari's equalizer on the stroke of half-time was the turning point, with Rahimi and Yassine sealing it in the second half. Morocco advance to the Round of 32.

title: Scotland vs Brazil - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Brazil 3-0, exactly as the pre-match read suggested. Vinicius Jr brace, Cunha on top, Guimaraes with two assists. Scotland's flat back four problem flagged before kickoff was exposed inside the first minute.

title: France vs Senegal - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: France 3-1 after a dire first half, then total transformation. Mbappe broke two records in one night — France's all-time scorer (58 goals) and equaling Fontaine's French World Cup record (14). Olise's half-time positional switch was the tactical fix that unlocked everything.

title: Argentina vs Algeria - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Argentina 3-0, but the result is secondary. Messi's first-ever World Cup hat-trick on his 200th cap, exactly 20 years after his debut, equaled Klose's all-time scoring record of 16. There is no version of this analysis that isn't about him.

title: Spain vs Cape Verde - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: One of the biggest shocks in World Cup history — Spain 0-0 Cape Verde. The reigning European champions had 27 shots and couldn't score, thanks to 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha producing a perfect 7-save performance on the biggest stage of his career.

title: Germany vs Curacao - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Germany 7-1 in an emphatic opener, but the headline belonged to Curacao — the smallest nation in World Cup history scored their first-ever World Cup goal through Comenencia, sending their fans into delirium even as the scoreline turned heavy.

title: Brazil vs Morocco - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Brazil 1-1 Morocco in a genuinely even contest. Saibari chipped Alisson after 21 minutes, Vinicius Jr struck back with individual brilliance on his 50th cap, and Alisson's stoppage-time double save prevented a Moroccan upset. Saibari registered 100 pressures — the tournament's joint highest.

title: Mexico vs South Africa - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Mexico 2-0 in a chaotic World Cup opener — three red cards, a 2010 rematch, and Raul Jimenez's first ever World Cup goal. Quinones punished a Sithole error after 9 minutes, Jimenez headed in the second, and the match descended into chaos from there.
